name: Mustard Seed Development Center
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor in Akron, OH offering free homebuyer education, financial counseling, and mortgage delinquency assistance to help residents avoid foreclosure.
description_long:
Mustard Seed Development Center (MSDC) is a non-profit community development organization based in Akron, Ohio that provides free financial and housing counseling services to Summit County residents. Founded to address housing instability and financial hardship, MSDC operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency with certified counselors on staff.

MSDC offers three primary service tracks: (1) Free homebuyer education and counseling through "HomeToday" webinars and online counseling to prepare first-time buyers; (2) Free financial empowerment counseling through "Money Matters" webinars and individual sessions; and (3) Mortgage delinquency and property tax assistance for homeowners facing foreclosure risk, including loan modification support and property tax repayment plan coordination with Summit County. All counseling services are provided at no charge except for lender-required online homebuyer certification ($100 fee paid to third-party E-Home).

MSDC distinguishes itself by offering income-based assistance specifically for delinquent mortgage and property tax situations, with clear eligibility guidelines and a structured application process. The organization works directly with lenders on modification plans, forbearance agreements, and foreclosure alternatives. Staff includes dedicated directors (Toree Stokes) and assistant directors (Maranda Brisco) with direct contact information, suggesting accessible personal service rather than call-center based support.

A key limitation is that MSDC explicitly states "No Down Payment Assistance Available," and delinquent property tax assistance is pending external funding from Summit County. Services are geographically limited to Akron/Summit County area. The organization functions as a non-profit counselor rather than a lender, meaning they facilitate but do not fund financial solutions.
pros: Free HUD-certified housing counseling and homebuyer education (no counseling fees)
Free financial empowerment counseling through structured "Money Matters" program
Direct mortgage modification assistance with lenders including forbearance and repayment plans
Delinquent property tax assistance program coordinated with Summit County government
Published income eligibility guidelines ($53,450-$100,750 for 1-8 person households)
Flexible scheduling including evening webinars (Monday/Thursday 6-8pm) and weekday appointments
Staff leadership directly accessible by cell phone and email for case management
cons: Explicitly does not offer down payment assistance despite serving first-time homebuyers
Delinquent property tax assistance funding is pending/uncertain ("waiting for financial assistance")
Geographic limitation to Akron/Summit County area only
Lender-required online homebuyer counseling incurs $100 non-refundable fee (paid to third-party E-Home)
Limited scope of foreclosure prevention (no direct legal representation, referrals only)
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Summit County preparing for purchase and lender-required counseling
Homeowners with mortgage delinquency or falling behind on property taxes seeking loan modification
Low-to-moderate income households (under $100K/year for 8-person household) facing housing instability
Consumers seeking free financial counseling and budgeting assistance in the Akron area
diagnosis: MSDC is best for Akron-area homeowners facing mortgage delinquency or property tax arrears who need HUD-certified counseling and lender modification support, or first-time homebuyers requiring free education and lender-mandated online certification. The main caveat is that MSDC provides counseling and facilitation only—they do not lend funds themselves, and down payment assistance and property tax assistance funding are not currently available.
services: Free homebuyer education webinars (HomeToday program, Mondays 6-8pm)
Free financial counseling webinars (Money Matters program, Thursdays 6-8pm)
Individual Zoom counseling sessions M-F 9am-6pm by appointment
HUD-certified housing counseling for mortgage delinquency
Mortgage modification plan assistance and negotiation with lenders
Foreclosure avoidance strategy guidance and legal referrals
Reverse mortgage and HECM counseling referrals
Delinquent property tax repayment plan coordination with Summit County
Income verification and documentation package assistance
Budget planning and financial intake assessment
Deed-in-lieu and refinance exit strategy counseling
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1557 Vernon Odom Blvd, Ste 201, Akron, OH 44320
